title: Copy-Assign a Label
prompt_md:
## Goal

Give a `Label` class (text plus an integer id) a custom copy assignment operator. After `a = b`, label `a` must hold a copy of `b`'s state and print identically.

## Input

- Line 1: a string `text1` (text of label `a`).
- Line 2: an integer `id1`.
- Line 3: a string `text2` (text of label `b`).
- Line 4: an integer `id2`.

## Output

Four lines: print `a` then `b`, then perform `a = b`, then print `a` then `b` again. Each label prints as `[id] text`.

Write `operator=` as a member that takes a `const Label&`, guards against self-assignment (`this == &other`), and returns `*this` by reference. A `print()` member is provided.

title: Chained Assignment
prompt_md:
## Goal

Give a `Counter` class a copy assignment operator that supports **chained** assignment, then perform `a = b = c` on three counters. Chaining works only because `operator=` returns `*this` by reference (the chain evaluates right-to-left: `a = (b = c)`).

## Input

- One line with three integers `va vb vc`.

## Output

- Print `a`, `b`, `c` (one value per line). Then a blank line. Then perform `a = b = c` and print `a`, `b`, `c` again.

Write `operator=` as a member taking a `const Counter&`, guarding self-assignment, and returning `*this` by reference. A `print()` member is provided.
